at_a_glance: You've spent .91 across 2 sessions on 2026-04-18. The planning session was /bin/zsh.61 and Phase 1 build was .30 — heavier than expected because 7 subagents were spun up via vibe-parallel, each carrying its own full context window. Phase 1 is complete with a clean gate (7.9/10 review score). Phase 2 features not yet started.

session_narratives.2026-04-18-001: Full planning session covering architect:, new-app:, and spec-review: in sequence. Produced ARCHITECTURE.md, SPEC.md, PLAN.md, CLAUDE.md, TASKS.md, and the full vibe/ folder. One P0 spec gap (missing mode system prompts) was caught and fixed before the build begins — a cheap fix at planning time versus an expensive one mid-Phase 2.
session_narratives.2026-04-18-002: Phase 1 complete in a single session using vibe-parallel. Wave 1 ran 4 subagents simultaneously (entitlements, main.js, preload.js, index.html). Waves 2–4 were sequential single-task agents (PATH resolution, global shortcut, IPC stubs). The phase-1-review caught 3 IPC contract bugs that were fixed inline — all 3 would have broken Phase 2 integrations if missed. The subagent approach was the dominant cost driver at ~/bin/zsh.64 of the session total.

recommendations.title: Single-task waves do not benefit from subagents
recommendations.body: Waves 2, 3, and 4 each contained exactly one task — so vibe-parallel spun up 3 agents with zero parallelism benefit. Each agent carries ~12,000 tokens of context overhead (prompt, ARCHITECTURE.md, task briefing). Running those tasks directly in the main session would save that overhead. Reserve subagents for waves with 2+ truly parallel tasks.

recommendations.title: Prompt caching not yet measurable — monitor in Phase 2
recommendations.body: Phase 1 was a single session so there's no caching baseline yet. In Phase 2, CLAUDE.md (~5KB) and ARCHITECTURE.md (~10KB) will be read at the start of every session. If these files stabilise after F-STATE, Anthropic prompt caching will reduce re-read costs by 90%. Avoid editing CLAUDE.md between tasks — every change resets the 5-minute cache TTL.

forecast_narrative: Phase 2 has 5 features remaining (F-STATE, F-FIRST-RUN, F-SPEECH, F-CLAUDE, F-ACTIONS). Each will generate 5–8 sub-tasks when planned. At the current rate of ~/bin/zsh.10/task, expect .50–2.50 to complete Phase 2, with F-CLAUDE (Claude CLI integration) likely the most expensive feature. Phase 3 polish adds another /bin/zsh.40–0.80. Total project estimate: .00–5.20. Fixing the single-task subagent pattern could trim /bin/zsh.30–0.50 from that total.
